title: Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view
design_brief: A custom scrollable view (canvas, map) accepting two-finger pan scroll, like iPad trackpad scrolling. Reference: Procreate canvas, Figma mobile.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single-finger scroll on canvas (conflicts with draw)
mobile_reasoning: Gesture.Pan().minPointers(2).maxPointers(2). On end, withDecay for momentum.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-151, define named constants for Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-151, isolate cross_platform differences for Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-151, test Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: Decay on release.
gesture_strategy: Two-finger Pan + simultaneous draw pen.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-151 (Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps continuous gesture math for Trackpad-style two-finger scroll on a custom view in gesture/Reanimated worklets, commits state once at release, and tests vertical-scroll coexistence plus reduced-motion fallback.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Multi-touch only. 2) Native decay physics. 3) Composes with single-finger drawing.
